Title: Arabidopsis seed mucilage secretory cells: regulation and dynamics

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for polysaccharide export and normal mucilage secretion
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ECH is required for polysaccharide exportation through the secretory/vesicular pathway to the apoplasm. 2. It is categorized as a mucilage secretion protein. 3. Mutant phenotypes implicate it in mucilage secretion defects.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for mucilage secretion via vesicular trafficking
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YIP4A is necessary for polysaccharide exportation through the secretory/vesicular pathway. 2. It functions with YIP4B in mucilage secretion. 3. Mutants display secretion-related mucilage defects.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for mucilage secretion via vesicular trafficking
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YIP4B is listed among secretion proteins required for polysaccharide exportation. 2. It participates in the vesicular pathway delivering mucilage components to the apoplasm. 3. Mutant phenotypes support its role in mucilage secretion.

1. MOR1 is included in the mucilage secretion category. 2. It is necessary for correct polysaccharide exportation and/or secretion-associated cell organization. 3. Mutant phenotypes place MOR1 in the MSC toolbox.

1. ROH1 is one of three proteins associated with the exocyst vesicular complex needed for secretion. 2. It contributes to polysaccharide export to the apoplasm. 3. Mutants show mucilage secretion defects.
1. EXO70A1 is an exocyst complex component necessary for polysaccharide exportation. 2. It functions in secretory trafficking to the apoplasm. 3. Mutants are defective in mucilage secretion/release.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: not present on cited microarray; reported in MSC studies

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SEC8 is an exocyst vesicular complex subunit required for mucilage secretion. 2. It is needed for polysaccharide exportation to the apoplasm. 3. Mutants show secretion-related mucilage defects.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: controls pectin methylesterification and mucilage properties
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLY1 controls the degree of pectin methylesterification. 2. It likely acts by recycling pectin methylesterases in the post-Golgi endomembrane system. 3. Mutant phenotypes show altered mucilage modification/release.

1. FEI2 controls stabilization of mucilage through cellulose microfibrils. 2. It acts together with SOS5 and COBL2 in the adherent mucilage layer. 3. Mutants exhibit impaired mucilage extrusion/stabilization.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for mucilage stabilization in adherent layer
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SOS5 is one of the proteins controlling stabilization of mucilage cellulose microfibrils. 2. It functions with FEI2 and COBL2 in adherent mucilage organization. 3. Mutants show mucilage extrusion/stabilization defects.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: not present on cited microarray; reported in MSC studies

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for mucilage stabilization and also controls cellulose microfibril production in RPW and columella
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COBL2 contributes to stabilization of mucilage, particularly the adherent layer. 2. It additionally controls cellulose microfibril production in the radial primary wall and columella. 3. Co-expression studies using FEI2 as bait helped identify COBL2 as the relevant family member.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; co-expression-guided candidate selection
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Has dual roles in mucilage stabilization and cell wall architecture.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: important for correct seed coat cell shape and cell wall synthesis affecting mucilage release context
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CESA2 is one of two cellulose synthase subunits characterized for MSC cell wall development. 2. It is important for correct seed coat cell shape though not fundamental for cellulose synthesis in other cells. 3. It functions redundantly with CESA5 and CESA9 in radial wall reinforcement and columella synthesis.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: important for correct seed coat cell shape and wall synthesis affecting mucilage release context
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CESA9 is a cellulose synthase subunit involved in MSC wall development. 2. It is important for correct seed coat cell shape. 3. It acts redundantly with CESA2 and CESA5 in radial wall reinforcement and columella synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: likely transfers galacturonic acid residues during pectic mucilage polysaccharide synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly contributes to mucilage polysaccharide formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GATL5 is listed as a core mucilage synthesis protein in the MSC toolbox. 2. It is described as a Golgi-localized glycosyltransferase enzyme involved in production of pectin/hemicellulose moieties of mucilage. 3. Mutant phenotypes support a requirement in Arabidopsis seed mucilage formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; localization evidence cited in review
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MSC/seed coat expression during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper summarizing previously published evidence; mechanistic assignment is presented as involved in mucilage synthesis rather than fully resolved biochemistry.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: likely adds galacturonic acid residues to pectic polysaccharides for mucilage production
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly contributes to mucilage polysaccharide formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAUT11 is included in the mucilage synthesis category of the MSC toolbox. 2. It is grouped with glycosyltransferase enzymes involved in pectin and hemicellulose moiety production. 3. Mutant phenotype implicates it in Arabidopsis seed mucilage biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: monosaccharide precursor supply for pectin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: supports cytosolic synthesis of monosaccharide precursors, particularly rhamnose, used for mucilage polysaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MUM4/RHM2 is a core mucilage synthesis gene in the MSC toolbox. 2. It is described as involved in monosaccharide cytosolic synthesis needed for pectin/hemicellulose moieties of mucilage. 3. Mutant phenotypes demonstrate impaired mucilage production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this review, exact catalytic detail is not expanded; function is summarized at the precursor-supply level.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: participates in synthesis of hemicellulosic polysaccharide components of seed mucilage
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly contributes to mucilage polysaccharide formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLA2 is listed among enzymes involved in mucilage synthesis. 2. The review states it is a Golgi-localized glycosyltransferase involved in production of pectin and hemicellulose moieties of mucilage. 3. Mutant phenotypes support a role in Arabidopsis mucilage formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; subcellular localization evidence cited in review

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: inhibits pectin methylesterase activity to control pectin methylesterification status after secretion
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: controls mucilage modification and release through pectin esterification state
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PMEI6 is a key mucilage modification protein in the MSC toolbox. 2. It acts after secretion in the extracellular space to regulate pectin methylesterification. 3. Natural ecotype mutants in PMEI6 show mucilage release phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; natural variant evidence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MSC/seed coat specific expression reported in cited studies
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a classical catalyst making the polymer backbone, it is treated here as a pathway-associated modification enzyme because it directly controls pectin chemistry.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: modifies secreted mucilage polysaccharides by hydrolyzing side-chain sugars in the extracellular matrix
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for proper mucilage maturation and release
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BXL1 is listed in the mucilage modification category. 2. It functions after secretion within the extracellular space. 3. Its activity is required for proper maturation/assembly associated with mucilage extrusion.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: modifies secreted mucilage polysaccharides in the extracellular space, likely removing galactosyl residues during mucilage maturation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MUM2/BGAL6 is a core mucilage modification gene. 2. It functions in the extracellular space after secretion to mature mucilage polymers. 3. Multiple naturally occurring ecotype mutants carry causal MUM2/BGAL6 alleles with mucilage release defects.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; natural ecotype mutant mapping

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: SAM-dependent methylation homeostasis affecting pectin methylesterification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: controls adenosine metabolism and SAM-dependent methylation homeostasis, thereby influencing pectin methylesterification of mucilage
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: affects mucilage modification via altered methylation homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ADK1 is listed among mucilage modification proteins. 2. The review states it controls homeostasis of SAM-dependent methylation. 3. Through this, ADK1 influences pectin methylesterification and mucilage properties.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: cellulose microfibril synthesis associated with adherent mucilage
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: synthesizes cellulose microfibrils/rays that stabilize the adherent mucilage layer
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes mucilage stabilization and also contributes redundantly to radial wall reinforcement and columella synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MUM3/CESA5 governs synthesis of cellulose microfibrils that stabilize adherent mucilage. 2. It also functions redundantly with CESA2 and CESA9 in radial primary wall reinforcement and columella synthesis. 3. Mutants show impaired mucilage extrusion/stabilization phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Has dual roles in mucilage stabilization and cell wall synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: radial primary wall loosening/breaking zone formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cell wall-localized peroxidase acting in radial primary wall microdomains associated with controlled rupture during seed rehydration
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for release of target product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PRX36 is characterized as a cell wall-localized enzyme in radial primary wall microdomains corresponding to the breaking zone. 2. prx36 mutants display a mucilage phenotype. 3. Natural ecotype variation also identified PRX36/PER36 causal mutations affecting mucilage release.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; localization analysis; natural variant evidence

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: wall enzyme activation/accumulation affecting radial wall loosening and swelling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: likely promotes accumulation and/or activation of cell wall-modifying enzymes needed for radial primary wall loosening or mucilage swelling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for release of target product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SBT1.7 is a cell wall-localized protease implicated in mucilage release. 2. The review proposes it triggers accumulation or activation of wall-modifying enzymes. 3. atsbt1.7 mutants show elevated PME activity and mucilage release defects.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; cell wall enzyme activity evidence

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: mucilage biosynthesis and release regulatory network
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: WD40-bHLH-MYB multimeric complex with EGL3, MYB5, TT2 and TT8
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: modulates expression of GL2 and TTG2 with AP2; regulates SBT1.7
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: master upstream regulator of seed coat differentiation and mucilage production/release
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for normal epidermal morphology and mucilage production/release
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TTG1 is part of the core WD40-bHLH-MYB complex controlling seed coat differentiation. 2. Together with AP2, this complex modulates two transcriptional subpathways through GL2 and TTG2. 3. TTG1 also regulates SBT1.7, supporting interconnected subpathways for mucilage extrusion.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; gene regulation studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in MSCs and also in other organs/embryogenesis contexts

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: mucilage biosynthesis and release regulatory network
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: WD40-bHLH-MYB complex with TTG1, MYB5, TT2, TT8
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: modulates GL2 and TTG2 expression with complex partners and AP2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major complex component for seed coat differentiation and mucilage regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for normal epidermal morphology and mucilage production/release
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EGL3 is a member of the WD40-bHLH-MYB multimeric complex. 2. This complex modulates GL2- and TTG2-linked transcriptional subpathways with AP2. 3. EGL3 is therefore part of the major regulatory pathway controlling mucilage development.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; protein interaction and regulation studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in MSCs and other tissues

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: mucilage biosynthesis and release regulatory network
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: WD40-bHLH-MYB complex with TTG1, EGL3, MYB5, TT2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: modulates GL2 and TTG2; activates/regulates SBT1.7
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major complex component controlling mucilage development
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT8 is part of the WD40-bHLH-MYB complex regulating seed coat differentiation. 2. With AP2, the complex controls GL2 and TTG2 subpathways. 3. TT8 also regulates SBT1.7, linking major subpathways in the mucilage network.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; gene regulation studies summarized in review

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: mucilage biosynthesis and release regulatory network
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: WD40-bHLH-MYB complex with TTG1, EGL3, MYB5, TT8
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: modulates GL2 and TTG2 expression with complex partners and AP2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key early seed coat TF used as bait in co-expression network; major regulator of mucilage developmental program
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT2 is a core MYB component of the WD40-bHLH-MYB complex. 2. This complex modulates GL2 and TTG2 transcriptional subpathways with AP2. 3. TT2 shows strong early seed coat expression and anchors co-expression patterns of MSC toolbox genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; co-expression and regulation studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highest expression in seed coat tissues at preglobular stage according to cited dataset

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: mucilage biosynthesis and release regulatory network
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: WD40-bHLH-MYB complex with TTG1, EGL3, TT2, TT8
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: modulates GL2 and TTG2 with AP2; regulates SBT1.7
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major regulator in seed coat developmental network for mucilage production/release
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for normal mucilage production and release
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB5 participates in the central WD40-bHLH-MYB complex. 2. The complex controls GL2 and TTG2 subpathways. 3. MYB5 also regulates SBT1.7, arguing against fully independent MUM1 and WD40-bHLH-MYB pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; gene regulation studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in MSCs and other organs

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: modulates expression of GL2 and TTG2 with WD40-bHLH-MYB complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: upstream regulator integrating transcriptional subpathways for mucilage production/release
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for normal mucilage development and seed coat differentiation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AP2 acts with the WD40-bHLH-MYB complex. 2. Together they modulate two subpathways through GL2 and TTG2. 3. ap2-based tissue separation studies also enabled transcriptomic mining of seed coat genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; regulatory evidence summarized in review

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: downstream transcriptional subpathway for mucilage production/release
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation downstream of WD40-bHLH-MYB and AP2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: one of two key downstream transcriptional branches of the major seed coat regulatory complex
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GL2 is a downstream transcriptional node controlled by the WD40-bHLH-MYB complex and AP2. 2. It defines one of two transcriptional subpathways in mucilage regulation. 3. Mutant phenotypes place GL2 in the MSC toolbox.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; transcriptional pathway evidence summarized in review

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: downstream transcriptional subpathway for mucilage production/release
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation downstream of WD40-bHLH-MYB and AP2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: one of two key downstream transcriptional branches in mucilage developmental network
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TTG2 is a major downstream transcriptional node of the WD40-bHLH-MYB complex and AP2. 2. It forms one of the two named transcriptional subpathways in the regulatory model. 3. Mutant phenotypes support a role in mucilage development.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; pathway evidence summarized in review

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation in a pathway considered independent of major master regulators
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: independent-pathway model discussed but considered likely interconnected
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PMEI6; BXL1; MUM2; PRX36; MUM4; PRX56; SBT1.7
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: differential regulation of multiple mucilage-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: important regulator controlling expression of several mucilage synthesis/modification/release genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MUM1 is identified as a recently analyzed regulator of mucilage development. 2. It was proposed to act independently of the other master regulatory genes. 3. The review notes that this independence may be overstated because SBT1.7 is also regulated by MYB5, TTG1 and TT8, suggesting interconnected pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis; target-gene regulation studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interconnection with WD40-bHLH-MYB pathway is emphasized.

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB61 is listed among transcription factors with roles during mucilage development. 2. It contributes to the regulatory network controlling seed coat mucilage. 3. Detailed target relationships are not specified in the review text provided.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: not present on the cited microarray; reported in MSC-related studies
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: MSC-related expression inferred from cited studies

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYB-like/DOF-related factor per cited study nomenclature
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: validated new candidate regulator from co-expression network analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DF1 is described as a newly analyzed regulator. 2. LASSO co-expression network analysis predicted DF1 as a mucilage-related candidate. 3. Phenotypic validation supported its role in Arabidopsis mucilage synthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: recently analyzed regulator of mucilage development
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DOF4.2 is included among the newer regulators analyzed in Arabidopsis mucilage development. 2. It is part of the MSC transcription factor toolbox. 3. Its precise pathway relationship to other master regulators remains unresolved in the review.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: not present on the cited microarray; reported in MSC studies

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NARS1 is listed among recently analyzed mucilage regulators. 2. It belongs to the NAC TF family implicated in seed morphology and mucilage development. 3. Direct relationships to the main known pathways were not yet demonstrated.

1. NARS2 is identified among newer seed mucilage regulators. 2. It is summarized as a TF involved in MSC establishment. 3. Direct network placement relative to other regulators remains unresolved in the review.
